TypeSignature.Value: .class public interface auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit IMultiThreadableTask implements class Microsoft.Build.Framework.ITask

Docs.summary: Interface for tasks that can execute in a thread-safe manner within MSBuild's multithreaded execution model. Tasks that implement this interface declare their capability to run in multiple threads within one process.
Docs.remarks:
The task must:
Use the provided for all modifications to process state such as environment variables, working directory, or process spawning instead of directly modifying global process state
Not depend on global process state for correct operation, including avoiding relative path resolution that relies on the current working directory
Use instead of for path resolution
Handle any internal synchronization if the task spawns multiple threads internally Tasks implementing this interface can be safely executed in parallel with other tasks or instances of the same task within a single MSBuild process, enabling better performance in multithreaded build scenarios.
See the Thread-Safe Tasks specification for detailed guidelines on thread-safe task development.

MemberSignature.Value: member this.TaskEnvironment : Microsoft.Build.Framework.TaskEnvironment with get, set

public: property Microsoft::Build::Framework::TaskEnvironment ^ TaskEnvironment { Microsoft::Build::Framework::TaskEnvironment ^ get(); void set(Microsoft::Build::Framework::TaskEnvironment ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the task execution environment, which provides access to project current directory and environment variables in a thread-safe manner.
Docs.value: Task environment which provides access to project current directory and environment variables.
Docs.remarks: This property is set on behalf of the Task by the MSBuild engine before the Task is executed. It should never be set by a Task itself.
